steps.title: Find the second factor using the constant term
steps.content:
If $(x + 2b)$ is a factor of $3x^2 + mx + 14b$, then:
$$3x^2 + mx + 14b = (x + 2b)(3x + c)$$
where $c$ is constant. The product of the constant terms equals $14b$:
$$(2b) \cdot c = 14b$$
Since $b > 0$, divide both sides by $b$:
$$2c = 14 \quad \Rightarrow \quad c = 7$$

steps.title: Expand to find the middle coefficient
steps.content:
Now expand $(x + 2b)(3x + 7)$:
$$(x + 2b)(3x + 7) = 3x^2 + 7x + 6bx + 14b = 3x^2 + (7 + 6b)x + 14b$$
The middle coefficient must be $7 + 6b$.

steps.title: Test which choice has the form $7 + 6b$ for positive integer $b$
steps.content:
For each choice, set the middle coefficient equal to $7 + 6b$ and solve for $b$:
  • A: $7 = 7 + 6b \Rightarrow b = 0$ (not positive)
  • B: $28 = 7 + 6b \Rightarrow b = 3.5$ (not an integer)
  • C: $42 = 7 + 6b \Rightarrow b = \frac{35}{6}$ (not an integer)
  • D: $49 = 7 + 6b \Rightarrow b = 7$ (positive integer) ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
